description_long: This object is a rugged, metallic briefcase that opens to reveal a built-in computer workstation. The upper half features a large widescreen display with an orange and black user interface, likely for technical or design work. The lower half contains a full QWERTY keyboard, a set of gold-colored buttons or sliders, and a joystick-like control, all embedded into a matte black surface. The exterior is constructed from a weathered, brushed metal, giving it an industrial and durable appearance suitable for field use. A sturdy, fixed handle is attached to the front for carrying, and the case appears to close securely with a central latch. There are no visible brand logos, external pockets, or corner reinforcements, emphasizing a minimalist, utilitarian design.
description: A rugged metallic briefcase with a built-in computer workstation, featuring a widescreen display, keyboard, and control panel inside a weathered metal shell.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open briefcase with a large display, keyboard, gold buttons, and joystick visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the case and the upright screen, with the handle partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, brushed metal exterior with no visible features or openings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the open configuration, keyboard, and control panel, with the handle visible at the front.
attribute_handle_type: Single fixed rectangular handle made of hard plastic or rubber, attached to the front with metal brackets; not retractable or telescopic.
attribute_lock_mechanism: Single central latch mechanism on the front, likely metal, with no visible keyhole or combination dial.
